tool_args.label: BETTERBUSES
tool_args.raw_text: BETTERBUSES

payload.description: MTA/NYC DOT bus priority program logo shown throughout presentation

tool_args.label: E.L. Grant Highway
tool_args.raw_text: E.L. Grant Hwy identified as top Bronx corridor. High ranking due to: High ridership & high volume of buses, Slow and unreliable bus service, Important cross-borough connections

payload.description: Bus priority corridor identified as top Bronx corridor; high ridership, slow bus speeds, important cross-borough connections

tool_args.label: E 167th St / E 168th St
tool_args.raw_text: E 167th St/E 168th St identified as top Bronx corridor. Bx35 carries 22,000 weekday riders. Average bus speeds: 4.7 mph AM peak, 4.3 mph PM peak. Key issues: Congested corridor w/ slow bus speeds, Double parking and loading, Vehicles blocking bus stops

payload.description: Bus priority corridor identified as top Bronx corridor; Bx35 route corridor with slow bus speeds, double parking and loading issues

tool_args.label: Bx35
tool_args.raw_text: Bx35 carries 22,000 weekday riders. Important crosstown connections to 8 bus routes and the 4, B/D Trains. Average bus speeds: 4.7 mph AM peak, 4.3 mph PM peak

payload.description: Bus route on E 167th St/E 168th St corridor; carries 22,000 weekday riders; average speed 4.7 mph AM peak, 4.3 mph PM peak

tool_args.label: Bx11
tool_args.raw_text: Bx11 service will run on E L Grant Hwy instead of Ogden Av, 168 St, and Shakespeare Av

payload.description: Bus route; after MTA redesign, Bx11 service will run on E L Grant Hwy instead of Ogden Av, 168 St, and Shakespeare Av

tool_args.label: Bx13
tool_args.raw_text: After redesign, E L Grant Hwy will carry over 26,000 daily passengers on the Bx11, Bx13, and Bx35

payload.description: Bus route on E L Grant Hwy corridor; after redesign will carry over 26,000 daily passengers on Bx11, Bx13, and Bx35 combined

tool_args.label: E.L. Grant Highway Bus Priority and Safety Improvements
tool_args.raw_text: Bx35 Bus Priority and Safety Improvements. Proposed Treatment: offset bus lanes, center running bus lane, bus boarding islands, physical protection. Benefits: Improves bus speeds and reliability, Improves bus stop accessibility, Shortens crossing distances, Enhances pedestrian safety, Improves bicycle safety, Reduces double parking or barriers to bus lane

payload.project_name: E.L. Grant Highway Bus Priority and Safety Improvements
payload.description: Proposed bus priority and safety improvements on E.L. Grant Highway including offset bus lanes, center running bus lane, bus boarding islands, protected bike lanes, pedestrian safety improvements, and intersection redesign at 170 St/Shakespeare Ave/Nelson Ave

tool_args.label: E 167th St / E 168th St Bus Priority Improvements
tool_args.raw_text: E 167th St / E168th St. Proposed Treatments: Queue Jump Signals, Segments of Bus/Bike Lanes, Curb Management. Proposed curbside bus lane from Jerome Ave. Proposed new meters and truck loading area.

payload.project_name: E 167th St / E 168th St Bus Priority Improvements
payload.description: Proposed bus priority improvements on E 167th St/E 168th St corridor including queue jump signals, segments of bus/bike lanes, curb management, and proposed curbside bus lane from Jerome Ave

tool_args.label: Connecting Communities: Harlem River Bridge Access
tool_args.raw_text: Edward L Grant Highway identified as priority project in the Connecting Communities Plan (May 2018). Protected bicycle lane improves bicycle and pedestrian safety and enhances access to/from Manhattan via Washington Bridge. Over 350 cyclists and 515 pedestrians use the Washington Bridge daily (7am-7pm). 3B Edward L Grant Highway / University Ave Create pedestrian and bicycle gateway to bridge as part of planned NYSDOT capital project. Upgrade existing bicycle route on E L Grant Hwy to protected path from E 167 St to Tremont Ave.

payload.project_name: Connecting Communities: Harlem River Bridge Access
payload.description: Edward L Grant Highway identified as priority project in the Connecting Communities Plan (May 2018). Protected bicycle lane improves bicycle and pedestrian safety and enhances access to/from Manhattan via Washington Bridge. Create pedestrian and bicycle gateway to bridge as part of planned NYSDOT capital project. Upgrade existing bicycle route on E L Grant Hwy to protected path from E 167 St to Tremont Ave.
